description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, exposing the dark interior or base material beneath the stone surface. The stone itself has a speckled, granite-like appearance with a mix of light beige, gray, and greenish hues, giving it a natural, rugged look. The sides of the block are solid and unadorned, contrasting with the highly textured top. The block is thick and robust, suggesting it is designed to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. Its construction and finish indicate durability and resistance to weathering.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and a speckled granite-like appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the grooves running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same grooved pattern across the top surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the solid, dark sides of the block.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or decorative obstacle
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained, with no moss or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or spaced as a standalone barrier
